ogTitle: Crafers West vs Craigburn Farm: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Crafers West and Craigburn Farm.
heading: Crafers West vs Craigburn Farm — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Crafers West, South Australia and Craigburn Farm, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Crafers West has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Craigburn Farm (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Crafers West or Craigburn Farm safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Crafers West has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Craigburn Farm scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Crafers West scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Craigburn Farm scores 65/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Crafers West or Craigburn Farm?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Crafers West has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
